meta.subject: Physics
meta.chapter: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

derivations.title: Conductivity of Intrinsic Semiconductor
derivations.priority_reason: Basic derivation connecting current density to mobility. Often appears as a 2-mark logical step or numerical basis.
derivations.diagram_description: Block of semiconductor with area A. Holes moving in direction of E, Electrons moving opposite. Both currents add up.

given_assumptions: Intrinsic semiconductor with electron density $n_e$ and hole density $n_h$.

steps.text: Total current is sum of electron and hole currents:
Using $I = nAev_d$. For electrons $v_e = \mu_e E$, for holes $v_h = \mu_h E$.
steps.latex: I = n_e A e (\mu_e E) + n_h A e (\mu_h E)

steps.text: By Ohm's law in point form $J = \sigma E$. Comparing:

examiner_notes: Crucial concept: Electron and Hole currents add up physically (opposite charge, opposite velocity = same current direction).
question_forms: Derive the expression for the conductivity of an intrinsic semiconductor in terms of carrier mobilities.
Show that the total current density in a semiconductor is sum of electron and hole current densities.

derivations.title: Rectification Efficiency of Half Wave Rectifier
derivations.priority_reason: Often asked if 'Derive the efficiency' is specified. Good for 3 marks involving integration.
derivations.diagram_description: Circuit with diode and load R. Input waveform (sine). Output waveform (only positive half cycles present).

given_assumptions: Input current $I = I_0 \sin \omega t$ for $0$ to $\pi$, $0$ for $\pi$ to $2\pi$.

steps.text: AC Input Power $P_{AC} = I_{rms}^2 (r_f + R_L) \approx I_{rms}^2 R_L$ (neglecting diode resistance):

examiner_notes: Integration limits are 0 to pi, not 2pi (current is zero in second half).
I_rms is I0/2 for half wave, not I0/root(2) (which is for full wave).
Result 40.6% is a standard value to remember.
question_forms: Derive the expression for the efficiency of a half-wave rectifier.

derivations.title: Rectification Efficiency of Full Wave Rectifier
derivations.diagram_description: Center-tapped transformer with two diodes D1, D2 and Load R. Waveforms: Input, D1 current, D2 current, Total Load current (unidirectional pulses).

given_assumptions: Current flows in both half cycles.
examiner_notes: I_rms calculation is the key difference from HWR.
Must show that efficiency is exactly double that of HWR.
question_forms: Derive the expression for the efficiency of a full-wave rectifier.
Show that the efficiency of a full wave rectifier is 81.2%.
